subject: Can you take two minutes to complete this survey?
body:
I look forward to seeing what we will accomplish together.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=790%2E150750%2EdLPt9e)

Friends -
Before diving in, I want to take a moment to introduce myself. My name is Faiz Shakir and I am deeply honored to be your campaign manager for the Bernie 2020 campaign.
This is a campaign built to win and planning to win from the very beginning. We have a strategic advantage that no other candidate has – the power of the people. And that is why we intend to listen to you and seek your feedback often.
[**Can you please take a quick minute to complete our survey below so I can get a good sense of your priorities as we build out this campaign together?**](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em-campaign-priorities-survey/?source=em190321-2-120&t=2&akid=790%2E150750%2EdLPt9e)
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em-campaign-priorities-survey/?source=em190321-2-120&t=3&akid=790%2E150750%2EdLPt9e)
In order to win, we must convince voters that Bernie is the candidate most likely to defeat Trump, and that he's the candidate we trust to do the things he says he will do when he's in the Oval Office.
We’re off to a good start, but before we go too deep into the race I wanted to take a moment to say hello, and to say thank you for everything you’ve done to put our campaign in a position not just to win the Democratic primary and defeat Trump, but to transform our country.
Thanks again for taking a minute to fill out this survey. We'll be in touch again soon.
Best wishes,
Faiz Shakir
We'd like to hear directly from you about why you support Bernie and what you think is required to win this primary, defeat Donald Trump, and transform our country.

**Take a moment to complete our brief survey to say what it will take to win.**

[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em-campaign-priorities-survey/?source=em190321-2-120&t=4&akid=790%2E150750%2EdLPt9e)

If you want to receive less emails, [just tell us here](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=790%2E150750%2EdLPt9e). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

We have an official FEC fundraising deadline coming up and we're trying to report as many individual donations as we possibly can. name: Mary Mancini
subject: All Leadership Starts Locally
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
# [](https://act.myngp.com/el/_5_lOKW78v4ZuYaVEe59uDMPj_ZVNO9CXLO8LhmD944=/brilBnFoyVPXE0NO8LuXoZrIs6W8ZmnnPJ7tBGedSj4=)
Dear Peter, On its face, the results of the 2018 election tell one story. Dig a little deeper, however, and they tell quite another. In the August 2018 primary election, Democratic voter turnout in Tennessee increased more than any other state in the country. In the November election, Democratic vote share increased by double digits in all 95 counties. Those increases led directly to Democrats flipping two seats in the state legislature, meaning that for the first time in more than two decades there are more Democrats in Nashville after an election than there were before it. But this trend isn't new. Since 2014, Democratic vote share for state legislative races has increased by about 15 percent. This change isn't surprising when you look at what Republicans have done to our state in the decade since they took control. The Tennessee Republican Party has wasted time and taxpayer money on divisive, hot-button issues and ignored the issues most important to Tennesseans, like health care, rural broadband, economic opportunity, opioid addiction and ineffective public schools. The increase in Democratic performance also shows that the work Tennessee Democrats and our allies have been doing to organize across the state at the grassroots level is working. It means the commitment to the fight for a better future for our people, our economy, our planet, and the soul of our country has become a statewide movement. It means the hard work of engaging people from every walk of life has been worth it. In 2019, there is only one way forward for all those who share our values: You must get involved now. You must join together with people from all walks of life to fight for our future, just like when we fought for and won better wages, safer workplaces and civil rights in our past. Your first opportunity is in just a few weeks at the local [**LEAD 2020: County Party Leadership Elections**](https://act.myngp.com/el/_5_lOKW78v4ZuYaVEe59uDMPj_ZVNO9CXLO8LhmD944=/B93JzSSUuTSNDoAA_1RD902EDX3QKBfyheJOTDWxsGk=) event in your home county. Since the work the TNDP does to elect Democrats -- from the city council chamber to the White House and every office in between -- starts at the county level, if you want to help Tennessee Democrats continue our forward momentum, your involvement in your county party is crucial. At each county LEAD 2020 event, Democrats will elect new officers (chair, vice chair, secretary and treasurer) as well as members of the executive committee, which takes care of the business of the county organization. Your leadership is what the Tennessee Democratic Party needs most. The Tennessee Democratic Party is led from the ground up and as a leader in your county, you know what's happening on the ground and can help us with candidate recruitment, finding and partnering with allied organizations, grassroots organizing and visibility at town halls and in the town square. Your next opportunity is ongoing, because elections happen all the time: [**Run for office**](https://act.myngp.com/el/_5_lOKW78v4ZuYaVEe59uDMPj_ZVNO9CXLO8LhmD944=/wFKcwPhJnXPaWO6GgQff2KDtCITuV172kMPJitl95oM=?subject=I%20am%20Interested%20in%20Running%20for%20Office). Across the country, people who don't look like the traditional politician are running for city council, county commission, school board and the state legislature -- and winning. Tennessee is no different. You are no different. You know your neighbors, friends and family and the problems and opportunities that exist in your own community. Find the problems you are most passionate about solving and the opportunities you are most passionate about growing, figure out which legislative body addresses these issues and opportunities and, in the words of Democratic Party organizer Amanda Litman, run for something! The Tennessee Democratic Party believes our government, at every level, must be smart, determined, resourceful and effective. With more Democrats like you elected to the state legislature, we can have affordable and accessible health care for all. With more Democrats like you elected to the city council, we can make investments in clean, sustainable water, energy and transportation. With more Democrats like you elected to the school board, we can fully fund public education so that every Tennessee student can get an education that fits their vision for a better future. With more Democrats like you elected to the county commission, we can join together to ensure every Tennessean -- no matter who you are, what you look like or where you live -- has everything you need to fulfill their hopes, dreams, aspirations and promise. There is no time to lose. Tennessee is at a crossroads and it's time to stand up, in your county or on the campaign trail, so together we can make Tennessee a place where everyone has the freedom to reach their full potential, no exceptions. Visit **[tndp.org](https://act.myngp.com/el/_5_lOKW78v4ZuYaVEe59uDMPj_ZVNO9CXLO8LhmD944=/B93JzSSUuTSNDoAA_1RD902EDX3QKBfyheJOTDWxsGk=)** for more information about LEAD 2020 and running for office in Tennessee. All the best, Mary
\--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

name: My Faith Votes
subject: SCOTUS Watch #11
body:
| | Critical Updates from the current session of the Supreme Court
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr0oT_KQ5AG8gbr0HbrBqP7M9FIe-UwaZvHVwVJCaLqQBQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190322com01&n=1)
|
#### Changes to SCOTUS? It’s Being Discussed.
Democrats in Congress are looking to expand the number of justices on the Supreme Court if they win in 2020. Some of the suggested proposals would add as many as ten new Supreme Court Justices to the bench and other proposals would impose term limits for life-tenured federal judges. It’s evident that the Supreme Court is becoming one of the Democrats’ main talking points gearing up for 2020. This isn’t the first time a change to the number of Justices on the Supreme Court has been proposed. In fact, the Constitution does not specify a set number of justices to the high court, so the decision is Congress’s to make. The first Supreme Court included six justices and for more than a century, since 1869, the Supreme Court has held nine justices.
### PRAYER ALERT
Please continue to pray for two important religious liberty cases, the American Legion v. American Humanist Association case heard by the Supreme Court last month. And the Coach Kennedy case that we hope will be heard during the next term.
#### American Legion v. American Humanist Association Case
The Supreme Court heard oral arguments for this case on February 27. The Court will determine more than whether crosses can remain on public property. The case will set a significant precedent for religious freedom as it relates to interpreting the Establishment Clause found in the First Amendment of the Constitution. Thank you for praying for the First Liberty attorneys who argued in this case. It appears that a majority of the justices will rule in favor of the cross, but we will not know until their vote is announced in June. Justice Gorsuch, in support of allowing the cross to stand, said “We accept that people have to sometimes live in a world in which other people’s speech offends them. We have to tolerate one another.” Continue to pray that the Justices rule in favor of religious freedom.
#### Kennedy v. Bremerton School District
Another critical case to pray for is First Liberty’s Coach Kennedy Case. You may remember, Coach Kennedy was suspended and terminated for silently praying on the football field after high school games. The Supreme Court decided in January not to review the Kennedy case, but there is a silver lining. [Click here to the rest of My Faith Vote’s article.](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr0oT_KQ5AG8gbr0HbrBqP7MgvRLWWgD_iRsCs4CdkTQbQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190322com01&n=2)
### SUPREME COURT CASE UPDATE FOR MARCH:
Below are two cases worth highlighting for which SCOTUS will hear oral arguments for this month.
#### Case 1: Rucho v. Common Cause
This case is gaining special attention, because it’s an issue the Supreme Court considered just last year. But this year, because Justice Brett Kavanaugh is now on the bench, things may play out differently. [Click here to the rest of My Faith Vote’s article](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr0oT_KQ5AG8gbr0HbrBqP7M40D-OnNTYOrTRPQ0rxMjzg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190322com01&n=3).
#### Case 2: Kisor v. Wilkie
Kisor, a retired marine suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder, filed a disability claim for benefits in 1983 with the Department of Veterans Affairs, and his claim was denied. In 2006 Kisor asked the VA to reopen his claim, and although they awarded the benefits, they determined he was not eligible for retroactive benefits dating back to 1983 when he first made the claim. [Click here to the rest of My Faith Vote’s article](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr0oT_KQ5AG8gbr0HbrBqP7M3PfuRbV5izidGZt3XneTqQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190322com01&n=4).

Democrats in Congress are looking to expand the number of justices on the Supreme Court if they win in 2020. Some of the suggested proposals would add as many as ten new Supreme Court Justices to the bench and other proposals would impose term limits for life-tenured federal judges. It’s evident that the Supreme Court is becoming one of the Democrats’ main talking points gearing up for 2020. This isn’t the first time a change to the number of Justices on the Supreme Court has been proposed. In fact, the Constitution does not specify a set number of justices to the high court, so the decision is Congress’s to make. The first Supreme Court included six justices and for more than a century, since 1869, the Supreme Court has held nine justices. Thank you for praying for the First Liberty attorneys who argued in this case. body:

| | |
# **‘ISIS Caliphate Has Crumbled and Last Stronghold Liberated’**

"The caliphate has crumbled, and the final offensive is over. While the official announcement hasn’t yet been made – Fox News has been told that this village, the last ISIS stronghold, is liberated," Benjamin Hall reports from Syria for Fox News.

“Troops here are now bringing down the black flags of ISIS. The flags no longer fly over the town, instilling fear.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2fa233af9d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump has recognized Israeli sovereignty over areas of the Golan Heights it currently controls. In doing so, Trump effectively formalizes something that everyone already knew. Namely, that the Golan Heights, which Israel seized following a 1967 Syrian-Egyptian-Jordanian effort to annihilate it, were unlikely to be Syrian again for a very long time,” Tom Rogan writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=248ac6892a&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| “President Trump on Thursday signed an executive order to promote free speech on college campuses by threatening colleges with the loss of federal research funding if they do not protect those rights,” Adam Shaw reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=acacf95f0a&e=3ee4985eea). “We’re here to take historic action to defend American students and American values,” the President said, surrounded by conservative student advocates at the White House. “They’ve been under siege.”
---
|
---
| “The man who killed a sheriff’s deputy and wounded a police officer in a central Washington state shootout was in the United States illegally,” _[The Associated Press](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1d525a08f7&e=3ee4985eea)_ reports. “The U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency said Thursday that 29-year-old Juan Manuel Flores Del Toro, who also died as a result of the shootout, was a citizen of Mexico.”
---
|
---
| The President’s “commitment to strengthening the military isn’t just making America safer; it’s also saving and creating jobs – including a whole factory’s worth of them right here in Ohio,” former Cincinnati Mayor Ken Blackwell writes in _[The Cincinnati Enquirer](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9d1558a3ca&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The manufacturing facility in Lima is a symbol of the economic revival taking place throughout Ohio and the rest of the country.”
---
|

body:
|
#
If we’re going to win this Democratic primary, take on this president, and win, we’re going to have to do it _together_. No corporate PACs propping us up, no federal lobbyists pulling the strings. Just real people in communities across the country working hard and fighting for our values. It’s important to our team that we know more about who you are and where you stand on the biggest issues facing our country. We also want to know whether you support Kamala for president, or whether you’re still deciding who to support in this primary. **Our team put together a survey for each of our supporters to learn more about what’s on your mind. Will you take a moment to fill it out?** **[Take a few minutes to complete the first survey of our historic campaign for president:**](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/1903-march-2019-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190322-full&source=ema190322-full&t=1&akid=3674%2E5755789%2EkNcg-J) | | [ Take the survey ](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/1903-march-2019-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190322-full&source=ema190322-full&t=2&akid=3674%2E5755789%2EkNcg-J)

| **_The Next Generation_** There’s no doubt that it’s a great time to be a Hoosier Republican. At your Indiana Republican Party, we’re committed to sustaining (and growing!) this unparalleled level of success.

And a key to that equation is the next generation. It’s the young adults coming through the ranks today. It’s our future party volunteers, our future county party officers, our future party staffers and our future candidates and elected officials.

That’s why over the last few months, our team has been visiting college campuses and talking with these future leaders of our party. We’re recruiting for our internships at state party ([read more about our open opportunities and how to apply here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=73d6f2790a&e=1acd9bb3d6)), but we’re also talking about what a great time it is to get involved in the Republican Party. This week our team was at Ball State, and recently we’ve been to Butler, IU, UIndy, IUPUI and Wabash. We’ll soon be visiting IU South, Franklin College and Anderson University.

For a young person getting involved today, opportunities abound. They could choose to get their start on a campaign, or perhaps they want to serve Hoosiers supporting an official anywhere from their local Courthouse to the White House! At the Indiana Republican Party, we’re committed to helping build our next generation. Will you help us make it happen? -Chairman Kyle Hupfer

**PHOTO:**_**Chairman Hupfer** introduces **Governor Eric Holcomb** at the Hamilton County Lincoln Day Dinner last week, before interviewing him in front of a packed house in Carmel._

| **_Governor Holcomb Launches 2019 Road Construction Season_** With this week’s official start of spring, **Governor Eric Holcomb** officially kicked off the launch of the 2019 Next Level Roads construction season! And for Hoosiers, this means big improvements are on the way for Indiana’s roads and bridges. In 2019, you can expect to see:

• More than 1,100 state construction projects
• More than $2 billion in investment
• 3,700 lane miles of pavement resurfaced/reconstructed
• 650 state bridges rehabbed/replaced
• 60 safety/traffic improvement projects

This is all part of **Governor Holcomb’s** commitment to long-term, sustainable investment into Indiana’s infrastructure. Through Next Level Roads, Indiana is investing more than $10 billion into construction and maintenance projects over the next five years and $30 billion over the next 20 years!

| **_Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge:
How's Your Bracket Doing?_** The games are ON! This week, **Governor Holcomb** [completed his bracket](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=651b6aed28&e=1acd9bb3d6), and for the second year in a row, he picked the Purdue Boilermakers to bring the national championship home to Indiana! So far, Governor Holcomb's bracket has had a rough early start - but there's still many games yet to be played.

Remember, the top three finishers at the end of our Beat the Governor Bracket Challenge will win either a basketball signed by **Governor Holcomb** or an item from our [Indiana GOP store](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=88b277a63e&e=1acd9bb3d6).

| **_Get to Know Logansport Mayoral Candidate Chris Martin!_** It’s a municipal election year in Indiana! We have Hoosier Republicans statewide who have stepped up to run for mayor this year – including **Chris Martin** , who is [running for mayor of Logansport](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1cb1687319&e=1acd9bb3d6), a city in Northern Indiana’s Cass County! He’s built several for-profit and non-profit organizations from the ground-up, and now he’s looking to bring his skills to improve his community.

Learn more about **Chris Martin** , candidate for Logansport mayor:
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d078f62f9a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
**Q: What’s your vision for Logansport in ten years?**
A: My vision for Logansport in 10 years would be a unified government with positive leadership and opportunities for generations now and in the future.

**Q: What makes Logansport unique?**
A: We’re an historic city and home to the Dentzel Carousel – a hand-carved carousel that’s more than 100 years old!

**Q: What’s your plan to help grow Logansport's local economy?**
A: We will grow our economy by reaching out to opportunities instead of expecting them to come to us. As mayor, I’ll work with our local economic development factions to increase opportunities for Logansport.
---
| **Q: What are your biggest priorities for Logansport if elected?**
A: We should be appreciating our history more and utilizing the resources we have, while expanding our opportunities abroad. We should also be exploring opportunities with other communities that are figuring out how to get the jobs Logansport has been wanting for years.

Are you a Republican mayoral candidate (_without a Republican primary challenger_) who would like to be featured in an upcoming edition of The Party Line? Contact us at [info@indiana.gop](mailto:info@indiana.gop), and we’ll be in touch soon!

| **_Lincoln Day Dinners: One Week, Six Dinners_** In just the last week, county parties across Indiana hosted six successful Lincoln Day Dinners in all corners of the state! They covered everywhere from Wells County (with **Senator Todd Young**!) in northeast Indiana to Vanderburgh County (with **Senator Mike Braun**!) in southwest Indiana, and everywhere in between. That includes big events in Hamilton, Madison, Montgomery and Rush counties with **Governor Eric Holcomb** and **Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer**!

Tonight’s Lincoln Day Dinner will be in Hancock County, with even more on the way next week! Check the full list of events below to see when your local Lincoln Day is scheduled!

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Will brave win?
body:
The way to beat a bully is to show him just how unafraid we are.
Peter,
With only a few days until the first FEC deadline of this campaign, my team says we've fallen behind our goals for the quarter. It would be a real setback--just as we're gaining momentum.
I'm writing right now to explain why investing in our campaign will help this country make the choice to be brave. And why it's so important you give now, before our first filing deadline and first chance to show the strength of our movement.
Donald Trump won on a message rooted in fear. He's pitted people against each other. He's given to corporations by taking from working families. He's chosen walls and torn down the bridges of trust that bind us.
Look, fear didn't lead to the New Deal. It didn't get us to the moon. It didn't pass the Civil Rights Act.
As president, I'll fight for a Green New Deal and Medicare for All. We need money out of our politics and an end to gun violence. These fights will be hard, but the well-being of our families and the future of our democracy depend on them.
**I 'm going to Trump International Hotel on Sunday to take our fight to Trump's doorstep. But it will only work if we have fundraising numbers to back it up. We've got the FEC deadline coming up, when all our numbers--revenue, donors--will be public. And we're still working to guarantee our spot in the debates.**
**[Click here to make a donation in any amount to help us get closer to our end-of-quarter fundraising goal and keep building this campaign. It's the single most important thing you can do to power our movement, raise our voices and defeat Trump.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976bfd/2ae112ef/125cc12f/357d7952/1331139256/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azI5MDgtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk2908-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
My first run for office was in a two-to-one Republican district. My advisors told me there were more cows than Democrats. I ran anyway--and I won.
I doubled down once I got into office, taking on fights others wouldn't and won't: Health care for the first responders who saved lives after 9/11. An end to Don't Ask, Don't Tell. I've stood up to Trump's attempts to deny transgender service members the ability to fight for their country.
As president, I will be brave, and I'll be brave from day one. Americans make hard choices all the time. You deserve a government that does the same. My administration would spend every day--every waking minute--working to live up to your trust and belief.
We have the power to choose what happens next. We can choose where we go from here. We can choose brave. You do that when you give to this campaign--and we're counting on it now, before the FEC deadline, which is coming up so fast.
**[Click here to give and power our campaign--$1, $5 or $10, whatever amount you can spare. You will take our movement to the debates. Through the primaries. To the White House.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976bfd/2ae112ef/125cc12f/357d7952/1331139256/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azI5MDgtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk2908-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
Thank you,

Kirsten

Peter,

The GOP majority in the Tennessee Legislature [made national headlines again](https://act.myngp.com/el/5y2axuqWGrJuDtHYEVLGkf5coMODB_PqZD0O58rgbJk=/SC4fUByEpzzR2HnUxa0z4gaJQB2ZiGvpcupHoph1yjo=?rref=collection%2Fcolumn%2Fmargaret-renkl&action=click&contentCollection=opinion&region=stream&module=stream_unit&version=latest&contentPlacement=1&pgtype=collection), this time with their excess of unconstitutional and extreme legislation attacking the autonomy of Tennessee cities and demonizing the least among us.

The [article](https://act.myngp.com/el/5y2axuqWGrJuDtHYEVLGkf5coMODB_PqZD0O58rgbJk=/SC4fUByEpzzR2HnUxa0z4gaJQB2ZiGvpcupHoph1yjo=?rref=collection%2Fcolumn%2Fmargaret-renkl&action=click&contentCollection=opinion&region=stream&module=stream_unit&version=latest&contentPlacement=1&pgtype=collection) concludes with a wake-up call for Tennessee and American voters:

The columnist is right.

Instead of hate-fueled political posturing, the majority of Tennesseans want safe communities where every family, through hard work, has the ability to create a bright and dignified economic future, where all children have an equally robust public education that propels them toward achieving their full potential.

You wear these values on your sleeve, and now more than ever, we need your help connecting this message to other Tennesseans.

[_Can you pitch in today to help deliver this unifying message to politically disconnected Tennesseans in communities across our state?_](https://act.myngp.com/el/5y2axuqWGrJuDtHYEVLGkf5coMODB_PqZD0O58rgbJk=/LRhANgPBCU06gyFnS8sgH8zi3Zrda9asfg3t_Kg2Q5A=)

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: A date we should all remember
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
Today is the 47th anniversary of the Equal Rights Amendment's submission to the nation's state lawmakers. While most anniversaries are cause for celebration, this one leaves me particularly frustrated.
| | Today is the 47th anniversary of the Equal Rights Amendment's submission to the nation's state lawmakers. While most anniversaries are cause for celebration, this one leaves me particularly frustrated.

As required for any Constitutional amendment to become law, the ERA requires ratification by 3⁄4 of all states to become law. When submitted on March 22, 1972, it seemed like we were well on our way there. Within five years, 35 of the required 38 states had ratified it.

Then, as quickly as it began, it stopped. It wasn't until 2017 that another state, Nevada, would ratify the ERA, with Illinois following up a year later.

But one state is still needed for the ERA to become the law of the land. We came close this past January here in the Commonwealth of Virginia, but despite a noble, concerted, and bipartisan effort to convince the General Assembly that women's rights are human rights, the ERA's ratification failed to receive the dignity of a vote in the House of Delegates despite passing the Virginia Senate for the sixth time since 2011.

For nearly half a century, the Equal Rights Amendment has languished in legislative purgatory. Today's anniversary is a reminder that we still have work to do if we're going to see this transformative piece of legislation added to our Constitution.

I look forward to celebrating that day.

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Beto O'Rourke, Kamala Harris, and Pete Buttigieg
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
It's already campaign season. Presidential candidates like Beto O'Rourke, Kamala Harris, and Pete Buttigieg are out there telling you about their platforms and, yes, asking for money before the public fundraising deadline at the end of this month.
That's good -- I'm thrilled that we have so many diverse and qualified voices vying for our party's nomination and no matter who our nominee is, the presidential race will be an expensive one.
**But a Democratic president can only be so effective without a Senate that supports him or her.** The Senate will confirm or reject our next President's Supreme Court nominees, and it will determine what bills comes to a vote.
We CAN win the Senate next year. We have amazing candidates running to unseat Republicans in states like Arizona, Colorado, and South Carolina, and I'm looking forward to seeing a strong Democrat challenge Mitch McConnell in Kentucky.
**Democratic Senate candidates need your support every bit as much as your preferred presidential ones do, everyone. It 's the only way we'll regain control.**
[**The first big public fundraising deadline of the year is coming up, and I 'd be so appreciative if you could please give $3 or whatever you can to support Senate Democrats. With so much at stake, a gift of any amount would be extremely meaningful.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2b8/2a380d31/3bd534bf/f10088/2415097676/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2gtNC1kc2NjIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxOTAzMjJfTVdINERTQ0NfNm1vIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh-4-dscc?refcode=e20190322_MWH4DSCC_6mo&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan

name: Kamala Harris
subject: BREAKING: The Mueller report is in. The American people have a right to see what’s in it.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
This news just broke: Special Counsel Robert Mueller has submitted his report to the Department of Justice. We don’t have many details yet, but here’s what must happen now. **We need total transparency. A declassified report must be made public immediately, and all underlying investigative materials should be handed over to Congress. Attorney General William Barr must also testify under oath before Congress about the findings of this investigation.** **[The American people have the right to know the truth. Add your name to my petition demanding the Mueller report be made public and that Barr testify before Congress»](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/1903-mueller?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190322-full&source=ema190322-full&t=1&akid=3677%2E5755789%2EJlN9CY)** | | [ SIGN THE PETITION ](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/1903-mueller?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190322-full&source=ema190322-full&t=2&akid=3677%2E5755789%2EJlN9CY)
---
The United States government owes the people the truth. This is about protecting our democracy and the people’s confidence in our elections. That’s why Attorney General Barr must testify under oath about the findings of this investigation.
You have my commitment that I will not rest until the truth is delivered publicly and in full.
I’ll be in touch soon as we learn more.
For The People,
— Kamala Harris

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: re: Mueller
body:
Peter--Special Counsel Robert Mueller has filed the results of his investigation into the Trump campaign's coordination with Russia, but this administration is doing everything it can to keep the truth hidden.
You deserve to know just how far Trump's corruption extends. You deserve justice and accountability. Most of all, you deserve a president who doesn't harm the country just to serve himself.
When I stand outside Trump International on Sunday and tell the president enough is enough, I want him to hear your voice right alongside mine. I want him to hear our voices every single day--to know, at every turn, that his agenda's days are numbered. That he'll never silence our calls for a stronger, brighter, more just future.
**Your voice matters --to me, to our campaign, to our future. So I'm asking you to make sure yours is counted by adding your name right now. **[Help us hit 25,000 people stating clearly that the future is ours, not Trump's. Add your name now to reject Trump's cruel agenda and join our fight for justice.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-muellersurvey?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2909-su-gfp-nd")****
| [ADD YOUR NAME:
Reject Trump's Agenda](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-muellersurvey?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2909-su-gfp-nd")
---
Trump knows he only wins when Americans are scared. And the way he tells it, there's a lot to be scared of. But his story is based on lies. Our movement is rooted in courage: the courage to believe that we _can_ pass a Green New Deal, get money out of politics and take on the gun lobby--and what's more, we _will_.
**On Sunday, Trump will hear us loud and clear. We will reject his politics of fear and corruption and choose a brave and bold path forward, and I 'm counting on you to be part of it. We need 25,000 people to sign on before midnight: **[Right now, add your name to reject Trump's hatred and fear and join the team that fights with courage for what's right.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-muellersurvey?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2909-su-gfp-nd")****
Thank you so much,
Kirsten
